proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, feature_078 is associated with pfs_months: higher feature_078 values are associated with longer pfs_months.
Among continuous features, feature_013 is associated with pfs_months: higher feature_013 values are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Among continuous features, feature_006 is associated with pfs_months: higher feature_006 values are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Among continuous features, feature_009 is associated with pfs_months: higher feature_009 values are associated with longer pfs_months.
Among ordinal features, feature_057 (3-level, values 0/1/2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months: higher levels predict shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.856 (p ≈ 0). Mean pfs_months rises monotonically across age deciles from 0.85 in the lowest to 6.86 in the highest decile.
Pearson r = -0.153 (p = 3.5e-260). Direction confirms higher feature_013 associates with shorter pfs_months.
Pearson r = -0.143 (p = 9.9e-226). Higher feature_006 → shorter pfs_months.
Pearson r = +0.123 (p = 1.5e-166). Higher feature_009 → longer pfs_months.
Mean pfs_months: feature_057=0 → 4.68; =1 → 3.49; =2 → 2.38. Spearman ρ = -0.386 (p ≈ 0). Strong monotonic decrease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, 55% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have lower mean pfs_months than feature_051=0.
Most binary features have NO meaningful association with pfs_months; only a small subset (e.g., feature_051, feature_109, feature_039) show significant differences in mean pfs_months between levels.

analyses.result_summary: Welch t-test: feature_051=0 mean pfs_months 4.031 (n=22519); feature_051=1 mean pfs_months 3.509 (n=27481). Difference = -0.521 mo, p = 1.6e-181. Confirms negative association.

analyses.code: univariate t-tests for all 77 binary features against pfs_months; sort by p
analyses.result_summary: Of 77 binary features, only feature_051 shows a large effect (|Δ|=0.52 mo, p=1.6e-181). feature_109 (Δ=+0.10 mo, p=2e-3) and feature_039 (Δ=+0.094 mo, p=3e-3) reach significance with smaller effects. Roughly 65 of 77 binary features show |Δ| < 0.05 mo and p > 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 is a constant column (n_unique=1) and provides no prognostic information for pfs_months.

After adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, and feature_051 in a single OLS regression, all six remain independently and significantly associated with pfs_months in directions consistent with their univariate associations.

analyses.result_summary: feature_007 takes the value 0 in all 50000 rows. Cannot be tested against pfs_months — it is constant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS (R²=0.946, n=50000). Coefficients: feature_078 +0.174 (p≈0), feature_057=1 -1.151 (p≈0), feature_057=2 -2.319 (p≈0), feature_013 -0.0027 (p≈0), feature_006 -0.076 (p≈0), feature_009 +0.479 (p≈0), feature_051 -0.376 (p≈0). All six predictors retain direction and remain highly significant after mutual adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is consistent across all three levels of feature_057 (i.e., feature_051 is associated with shorter pfs_months in feature_057=0, 1, and 2 strata).
There is a (small) interaction between feature_051 and feature_057: the absolute magnitude of the feature_051 effect on pfs_months attenuates as feature_057 increases.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_051 within each feature_057 level
analyses.result_summary: feature_057=0: Δ=-0.559 mo (p=7e-84); feature_057=1: Δ=-0.514 mo (p=5e-105); feature_057=2: Δ=-0.472 mo (p=9e-31). Negative effect is consistent in direction and significance across all strata.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) * feature_051 + feature_013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: feature_051 × feature_057=1 = +0.026 (p=0.006); feature_051 × feature_057=2 = +0.056 (p<0.001). Confirms small attenuation of feature_051 effect at higher feature_057 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is modified by feature_013: the negative effect of feature_051 is more pronounced in patients with high feature_013 than in patients with low feature_013.
Outside of feature_013, no other continuous biomarker shows a meaningful interaction with feature_051 on pfs_months at p < 0.01.

analyses.code: df['f013q']=pd.qcut(df.feature_013,4); per-quartile adjusted OLS for feature_051 effect
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_051 coefficients by feature_013 quartile: Q1 (mean f013=3.6) -0.042; Q2 (10.5) -0.032; Q3 (26.1) -0.042; Q4 (130.5) -0.149. Effect is ~3-fold larger in highest feature_013 quartile. Formal interaction term in joint OLS: feature_013:feature_051 = +0.0153 (p≈0), reflecting that the per-unit feature_013 hazard is partly offset when feature_051=1.

analyses.code: scan of feature_051 × continuous biomarker interaction p-values
analyses.result_summary: Among 33 continuous features, only feature_013 reaches p<0.01 for the feature_051 interaction (p≈0). feature_031 (p=0.011), feature_006 (p=0.024), and feature_009 (p=0.035) reach p<0.05 with small magnitudes. All other continuous features have interaction p > 0.06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months does NOT differ across racial groups (feature_018 levels: asian/black/hispanic/white/other) — i.e., there is no detectable racial outcome disparity in this cohort.
Mean pfs_months does NOT differ across insurance types (feature_045 levels: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by race: asian 3.797, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749, white 3.741. ANOVA F small, all pairwise differences < 0.06 mo. After adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_051, race coefficients remain ns (all p > 0.5).
Mean pfs_months by insurance: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA ns; adjusted insurance coefficients all p > 0.16 with magnitudes < 0.01 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The probability of receiving feature_051 (the apparent treatment) does NOT vary by race (feature_018) — i.e., no treatment-receipt disparity by race.
The probability of receiving feature_051 does NOT vary by insurance type (feature_045).
Higher feature_013 is associated with higher probability of receiving feature_051, suggesting feature_051 receipt is driven by disease severity (feature_013) rather than demographics.

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 rate by race: asian 0.549, black 0.554, white 0.550, hispanic 0.544, other 0.544. Chi² = 1.55, p = 0.82. No racial disparity in feature_051 receipt.
feature_051 rate by insurance: medicare 0.546, private 0.550, medicaid 0.555, uninsured 0.562. Chi² = 3.13, p = 0.37. No insurance-based disparity.

analyses.code: smf.logit('feature_051 ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_013 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 coefficient on log-odds(feature_051) = +0.0613 (p ≈ 0). feature_078 (p=0.70), feature_057 (p>0.78), feature_018 (all p>0.21), feature_045 (all p>0.37) all ns. Treatment receipt is driven primarily by feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient demographics (race, insurance, age) are NOT meaningfully associated with continuous biomarkers (feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_092, feature_038, feature_054, feature_062, feature_120, feature_118, feature_056) in this cohort.
The distribution of feature_057 (likely a stage/risk strata) is uniform across racial groups.

analyses.code: ANOVA of each continuous biomarker over feature_018 levels
analyses.result_summary: ANOVA p-values across racial groups: feature_013 p=0.67, feature_009 p=0.47, feature_006 p=0.32, feature_092 p=0.67, feature_038 p=0.63, feature_054 p=0.43, feature_062 p=0.78, feature_120 p=0.96, feature_118 p=0.66, feature_056 p=0.46. Insurance-vs-age p=ns (all p>0.6). No demographic-biomarker associations.
Race × feature_057 contingency: each race shows feature_057 distribution ≈ 35% level 0, 49–50% level 1, 15% level 2. Chi² p = 0.84. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, ~10% prevalence) is independently associated with longer pfs_months in adjusted analysis.
feature_039 (binary, ~10% prevalence) is independently associated with longer pfs_months in adjusted analysis.
Neither feature_109 nor feature_039 modifies the effect of feature_051 on pfs_months (no interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_109 coefficient = +0.154 mo (p = 2.3e-109) in the full-features OLS model. Confirms independent positive association.
feature_039 coefficient = +0.146 mo (p = 2.1e-100) in the full-features OLS model.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_051 * feature_109', data=df).fit(); same for feature_039
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_109 coefficient = +0.0012 (p=0.93). feature_051:feature_039 coefficient = -0.0045 (p=0.74). No effect modification of the feature_051 effect by either feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (5-level, values 6–10, plausibly Gleason score) is monotonically associated with pfs_months: higher feature_067 → shorter pfs_months.
Of the five 0–4-coded ordinal features (feature_016, feature_071, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064), none is monotonically associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_067: 6→3.71, 7→3.76, 8→3.74, 9→3.74, 10→3.70. No monotonic gradient. In the full multivariable model, C(feature_067)=8 coef -0.022 (p=4e-3); =9 coef -0.022 (p=8e-3); =10 coef -0.023 (p=4e-2). All effects are tiny (<0.03 mo). Hypothesis (monotone strong association) refuted.

analyses.code: spearmanr and groupby for each ordinal
analyses.result_summary: Spearman ρ between each 0–4 feature and pfs_months: feature_016 ρ=-0.005 (p=0.26); feature_071 ρ≈+0.004; feature_096 ρ≈-0.0003; feature_033 ρ≈-0.0003; feature_064 ρ≈+0.0002. Mean pfs_months differs by < 0.10 mo across levels for each. None shows a meaningful monotonic association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 (11-level, 0–10) is NOT meaningfully associated with pfs_months — it is a non-prognostic feature.
Most of the 77 binary features (~80%) are statistically null with respect to pfs_months in a comprehensive multivariable model — only a small subset (≤10) carry signal.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_026 ranges narrowly from 3.69 (level 5) to 3.84 (level 10). Spearman ρ ≈ +0.005 (ns). No prognostic signal.

analyses.code: Comprehensive OLS with all 120+ features; count of p>0.5
analyses.result_summary: Of 155 model parameters, 68 have p > 0.5 — these features and category levels contribute essentially no information about pfs_months. The truly significant predictors collapse to ~9: feature_078, feature_057 (both levels), feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_051, feature_109, feature_039, feature_092 (small but p<1e-80).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between feature_078 and pfs_months is non-linear: a quadratic term in feature_078 significantly improves model fit.
The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months becomes more pronounced (more negative) as feature_078 increases — i.e., a feature_078 × feature_051 interaction with negative sign.

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic term on feature_078: coef = +0.000846 (p ≈ 0). Adding it lifts R² from 0.946 → 0.949. Linear term coef changes from +0.174 (linear-only) to +0.064 (with quadratic). Confirms accelerating positive relationship with age-like variable.

feature_078 × feature_051 = -0.00427 (p ≈ 0). Negative-sign confirms feature_051 effect grows more harmful as feature_078 increases. Adding age² × feature_051 (coef +0.000378, p≈0) shows the effect is U-shaped with respect to feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_057 (stage/strata) and pfs_months does NOT differ by race (no race × feature_057 interaction).
The effect of feature_051 (treatment) on pfs_months does NOT differ by insurance type (feature_045).
The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months does NOT differ by race.

analyses.result_summary: All eight feature_057 × feature_018 interaction coefficients are ns (p ranges 0.33 to 0.99, all |coef| < 0.04). No race-stratified differences in the staging effect.
feature_051 × insurance interaction terms all ns (p=0.64, 0.80, 0.64); coefficients all |·|<0.012. No insurance-modulated treatment effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_051 * C(feature_018)', data=df); stratified t-tests by race
analyses.result_summary: Stratified analyses: white Δ=-0.525, black Δ=-0.506, hispanic Δ=-0.529, asian Δ=-0.535, other Δ=-0.366. All highly significant in direction; differences across races small. Formal interaction terms p ranged 0.22–0.90, all ns. No race-modulated treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092, despite a small per-unit effect, contributes independent and significant negative information about pfs_months in the comprehensive model (i.e., it is a real but minor prognostic factor).
Most binary features that show a marginally significant univariate association with pfs_months (e.g., feature_105, feature_043) do NOT survive adjustment in a comprehensive model.

analyses.result_summary: feature_092 coef = -0.000528 mo per unit (p = 4.3e-82). Per-unit effect is very small but the broad range (48–830, sd=80) means a 100-unit shift = -0.05 mo. Confirmed minor prognostic factor.

analyses.code: compare univariate p < 0.05 binary features to comprehensive model p
analyses.result_summary: feature_105 (univariate Δ=-0.092, p=0.015) drops to p=0.45 adjusted; feature_043 (univariate p=0.034) drops to p=4.4e-5 adjusted with coef -0.021 (sign preserved but very small). Most marginal univariate associations evaporate after adjustment for the dominant predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the entire cohort, the dominant prognostic structure for pfs_months is captured by ~9 features (feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_051, feature_109, feature_039, feature_092); the remaining ~110 features carry essentially no incremental signal.

Neither demographic features (race, insurance) nor most clinical features show outcome disparities or treatment-receipt disparities in this cohort, despite ample sample size — i.e., the dataset displays demographic balance with respect to outcomes and care.

analyses.code: fit reduced OLS with only the 9 candidate features; compare R² to full-features model
analyses.result_summary: Reduced 9-predictor model R² ≈ 0.946 vs full 120+ feature model R² ≈ 0.948. Adding all remaining features improves R² by only 0.002 — confirms the prognostic signal is concentrated in <10 variables.

analyses.code: summary across iterations 6–8, 13: race/insurance vs outcome, treatment, biomarkers, stage
analyses.result_summary: All race-vs-outcome, race-vs-treatment, race-vs-biomarker, race-vs-stage, insurance-vs-outcome, insurance-vs-treatment tests yield p > 0.2. The full 50,000-patient cohort shows no detectable demographic disparity on any axis tested. This is unusual for a real-world prostate cancer cohort and suggests the dataset is demographically balanced by construction.
